name: glossary
context: Ethereum terminology — definitions for agents and developers
info: Quick-reference glossary of Ethereum-specific terms. Agents can query individual terms via section addressing.

sections.title: ABI
sections.body: Application Binary Interface. A JSON description of a contract's functions, events, and errors. Required to encode/decode calls to a deployed contract. Generated by the Solidity compiler.

sections.title: EOA
sections.body: Externally Owned Account. A regular wallet controlled by a private key, as opposed to a contract account. EOAs initiate transactions; contracts cannot.

sections.title: EVM
sections.body: Ethereum Virtual Machine. The runtime that executes smart contract bytecode. Stack-based, deterministic, sandboxed. All EVM-compatible chains (Base, Optimism, Arbitrum, Polygon) run the same instruction set.

sections.title: Gas
sections.body: Unit of computation on the EVM. See [[ethereum/gas]] for full explainer.

sections.title: Gwei
sections.body: 10^9 wei = 1 gwei. The standard unit for expressing gas prices. 1 ETH = 10^9 gwei = 10^18 wei.

sections.title: Nonce
sections.body: A per-account transaction counter. Each transaction from an EOA must have a nonce exactly 1 higher than the previous. Prevents replay attacks and determines transaction ordering.

sections.title: RPC
sections.body: Remote Procedure Call. The JSON-RPC API that nodes expose for interacting with the chain. Methods include eth_sendTransaction, eth_call, eth_getBalance. Providers like Alchemy and Infura host RPC endpoints.

sections.title: Wei
sections.body: The smallest denomination of ETH. 1 ETH = 10^18 wei. Named after Wei Dai.

sections.title: Calldata
sections.body: The data payload of a transaction. For contract calls, this contains the function selector (first 4 bytes of the keccak256 hash) plus ABI-encoded arguments. Calldata is immutable and cheaper than memory or storage.

sections.title: Bytecode
sections.body: The compiled output of a Solidity/Vyper contract. Two types: creation bytecode (runs once during deployment, returns runtime bytecode) and runtime bytecode (stored on-chain, executed on every call).

author: dojo-community
